claim: A methodology change should be promoted to shared lab doctrine only after two independent wins in different chip contexts.
mechanism: Single-chip gains often reflect local evaluator bias, scaffolding quirks, or unusually friendly evidence. Requiring a second independent win forces the lab to test whether the mechanism survives domain shift before it becomes a default recommendation. That promotion discipline turns packet libraries into a compounding portfolio asset instead of a scrapbook of anecdotes.
boundary: This promotion rule governs durable shared doctrine. Local tactics and emergency fixes may still be adopted provisionally before cross-chip proof exists.
